---
title: "Reference Library"
---

## Core workflow references

| Resource | Use it for |
|---|---|
| [R for Data Science (2e)](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/) | End-to-end R workflow habits, especially projects, communication, and getting help |
| [R4DS: Workflow: scripts and projects](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/workflow-scripts) | Source of truth, project structure, and relative paths |
| [R4DS: Communicate](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/communicate) | Why Quarto, reports, and communication are part of the workflow |
| [R4DS: Workflow: getting help](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/workflow-help.html) | Reprexes and better support requests |

## FASRC and Harvard infrastructure

| Resource | Use it for |
|---|---|
| [FASRC Docs: Open OnDemand Dashboard and Remote Desktop](https://docs.rc.fas.harvard.edu/kb/virtual-desktop/) | Browser-based access, interactive apps, and session management |
| [FASRC Docs: R and RStudio](https://docs.rc.fas.harvard.edu/kb/r-and-rstudio/) | Current guidance on RStudio Server, package installs, and app limitations |
| [FASRC Docs: Scratch](https://docs.rc.fas.harvard.edu/kb/policy-scratch/) | Current retention and usage policy for `/n/netscratch` |

## NSAPH handbook pages worth copying from

Even if your lab is not NSAPH, these pages are useful models for practical documentation:

| Resource | Use it for |
|---|---|
| [NSAPH Handbook: How to work on FASSE](https://nsaph.github.io/handbook/fasse.html) | Secure-environment onboarding, project setup, shared data, scratch, and GitHub habits |
| [NSAPH Handbook: Getting started on CANNON](https://nsaph.github.io/handbook/cannon.html) | Standard-cluster onboarding |
| [NSAPH Handbook: Introduction to NSAPH GitHub](https://nsaph.github.io/handbook/analysis.html) | GitHub defaults for shared lab work |
| [NSAPH Handbook: Workflow Documentation](https://nsaph.github.io/handbook/docs.html) | Data dictionaries, diagrams, and README expectations |
| [NSAPH Handbook: Data Sharing using Harvard Dataverse](https://nsaph.github.io/handbook/dataverse.html) | Sharing and publishing data products when policy allows |

## Publishing and documentation tooling

| Resource | Use it for |
|---|---|
| [Quarto websites documentation](https://quarto.org/docs/websites/) | Building lab websites and internal handbooks |
| [Quarto notebooks documentation](https://quarto.org/docs/computations/r.html) | R-based notebooks and executable reporting |
| [Mermaid documentation](https://mermaid.js.org/) | Inline process and data-flow diagrams |

## Suggested order for new students

1. Read `Start Here`.
2. Read the R4DS workflow chapters.
3. Read the FASRC docs page for the environment you will actually use.
4. Read the relevant NSAPH handbook page that matches your project type.
5. Return to this site when you need a lab-default answer instead of a generic answer.

## If you only bookmark five links

1. [R4DS: Workflow: scripts and projects](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/workflow-scripts)
2. [FASRC Docs: Open OnDemand Dashboard and Remote Desktop](https://docs.rc.fas.harvard.edu/kb/virtual-desktop/)
3. [FASRC Docs: R and RStudio](https://docs.rc.fas.harvard.edu/kb/r-and-rstudio/)
4. [NSAPH Handbook: How to work on FASSE](https://nsaph.github.io/handbook/fasse.html)
5. [NSAPH Handbook: Workflow Documentation](https://nsaph.github.io/handbook/docs.html)
